how_verified: Verified by use against the LIVE card (chat-4b + embed-bge resident, ~5.3 GB free) without evicting/loading anything heavy. (1) needs-evict + no-auth -> the structured ASK: drove ensure_resident('chat-2b', evict=False) with the REAL registry + REAL measured VRAM (chat-2b needs ~7.37 GB > 5.36 free) and POISONED actuators (gpu.teardown/systemd.control raise if reached) -> returned {swap_needed:true, would_load:'chat-2b', would_evict:['embed-bge'] (the real largest-first plan), free_gb:5.36, needed_gb:7.37, approve:'re-call with ensure_evict=true', resident:false, evicted:[], message:'SWAP NEEDED...'} -- no exception, no evict, actuators never reached; same drive via model-id 'Qwen/Qwen3.5-2B' (the JOIN keying) also returns the ASK. (2) needs-evict + evict=True -> the existing authorized path: recorder stubs (no real systemd) -> action='evicted-and-loaded', teardown executed EXACTLY the plan the ASK offered ([embed-bge]), one start issued. (3) fits -> loads unchanged: tts-kokoro (0.9 GB < free) with stubs -> action='loaded', no eviction, no ASK. (4) already-resident no-op: fully REAL call ensure_resident('chat-4b') -> action='already-resident'. (5) impossible fit (mocked free=100 MB, chat-nemotron 14.4 GB > full-plan 13.0 GB) STILL RAISES EnsureResidentError under BOTH evict=False and evict=True (an ask whose approve would fail is a false offer). (6) resident set identical before/after every drive + `company gpu` unchanged (10.4 GB used, chat-4b + embed-bge). Existing suites all pass post-change: tests/model_capabilities_acceptance.py (172), tests/model_catalog_acceptance.py (41), tests/coherence_cli_acceptance.py (7), tests/mcp_engine_acceptance.py (35, incl. the LIVE embed + the ensure=False fail-loud check). No existing test pinned the old raise-on-evict=False, so no caller contract broke (same dict-or-EnsureResidentError signature; new return is the additive 'swap-approval-needed' action).
needs_tim: None on the design itself -- this IS Tim's 2026-06-09 spec, implemented as specified (ASK shape exact: swap_needed/would_load/would_evict/free_gb/needed_gb/approve). Two reported seams are the LEAD's to apply (out of this lane's files): see notes.
notes: WHAT CHANGED (ops/cli/capabilities.py, the #50 actuator's eviction-decision point): the needs-eviction branch now computes the largest-first plan (gpu.plan_eviction -- the ONE planner, reused) EITHER WAY; unauthorized (evict=False) RETURNS the structured swap-approval ASK carrying the REAL plan the authorized path would execute (never a hard-block, never a silent evict); authorized (evict=True) executes the SAME plan unchanged. The raise is now reserved for the genuinely impossible: no local service / can't fit even after the FULL plan (raises under EITHER authorization -- previously evict=False raised before even computing the plan, so this is strictly MORE truthful, same exception type) / failed start / never served. Docstring + the #50 comment block + ops/AGENTS.md models/VRAM type-view sentence (the C9.4 self-description home) updated by integration. NEEDED-SEAM 1 (runtime/cognition.py ~line 282-285, the run_role(ensure=) seam -- lead applies, NOT edited here): `_ensure_embedder_resident(evict=ensure_evict)`'s return value is currently DISCARDED; with G14, a swap-needing embedder load under ensure=True/ensure_evict=False returns the ASK, which run_role would swallow and then hit complete_embeddings' FabricError (still fail-loud, but the ASK is lost). TINY pass-through: `_res = _ensure_embedder_resident(evict=ensure_evict)` then `if _res.get("swap_needed"): return _res` (the MCP face then surfaces it with ZERO change -- mcp_face passes run_role's dict through). Also amend cognition.py:192's docstring ('Raises ... if it can't be made resident' -> '... or returns the G14 swap-approval ASK when eviction needs approval'). NEEDED-SEAM 2 (ops/cli/app.py:215-224, the `company ensure` operator face -- not in this lane's FILES, so reported not edited): on the ASK it now prints '✓ SWAP NEEDED -- ...' and exits 0; the message is loud but the check-mark + exit-0 could mislead a script into thinking the load happened. One-branch fix: `if res.get("swap_needed"): print("  ⇄ " + res["message"]); sys.exit(3)` (a distinct exit code = 'ask, not done'). FOLLOW-UP (test drift home): a G14 case (ASK on needs-evict-no-auth + the impossible-fit still-raises pair) belongs in tests/model_capabilities_acceptance.py per C9.4; tests/ wasn't in this lane's FILES -- the verify script content above is ready to fold in. NOT committed (per lane instruction); SYSTEM-GAPS.md G14 entry left for the lead to flip to closed with the commit sha.
